b. Remit all taxes withheld within the reglementary period to avoid possible imposition of penalty/surcharge; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: No request for write-off was filed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct all officials/employees concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. 

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials and employees to strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.

These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.
observations.reason: All employees were directed and ordered by the LCE to liquidate their cash advances but several cash advances remained unliquidated at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical inventory count; and

b. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit RPCPPE and reconcile the same with the records in the General Services Officer (GSO) and Accounting Office, and promptly submit a copy to COA for reference in audit.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prioritize the preparation and submission of the required BRS; and

b. Maintain SL for each bank accounts, thoroughly review the Cash in Bank records, and make any necessary adjustments to ensure accuracy in the FS and other documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

a. Exhaust all available remedies to collect or recover the dormant accounts from various AOs, otherwise, file a request for write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008 and its Annexes;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to those AOs who are still connected with the LGU and other liable persons with known whereabouts; and

c. Strengthen the internal control of the LGU with regard to granting, liquidation and monitoring of cash advances in order to prevent the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances particularly the dormant accounts.

We recommended that the LCE demand from the MT, LO and other AOs to immediately deposit their accountabilities as of December 31, 2024, submit the corresponding Report on Collections and Deposits (RCDs), deposit slips and updated cashbooks. Henceforth, require them to timely deposit their collections to the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB), in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory using the Perpetual Inventory System and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts; and

b. Create a functional Inventory Committee whose members are capable and with available time to perform the physical count. Require the Committee also to promptly prepare and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant for recording and reconciliation purposes, and to COA for audit verification.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted misstatement. Also, ensure that the Municipality is compliant with the accounting guidelines set out for the grants received from the NGAs in subsequent transactions.

We recommended that Management ensure in the succeeding years that the transfer of actual cash pertaining to the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the STF be made immediately at year-end.

We recommended that Management require the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to devise measures and exert more effort to expedite the preparation and submission of the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We request that such activities be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review. Further, direct the personnel responsible for accepting deliveries to notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries, furnish copies of AIRs within 24 hours upon acceptance and submit report on deliveries received and issued as of year-end.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the noted lacking documentary requirements to the Office of the Auditor for verification and ensure that supporting documents of the financial transactions are complete and properly accomplished before processing payments.

We recommended that Management submit all the lacking supporting documentary requirements, as required under Sections 9 COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to the Audit Team for validation. Likewise, ensure strict compliance on the completeness of the supporting documentary requirements before every DV be approved for payment in order to ascertain the propriety and validity of all claims against the government funds.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the officer concerned to prepare the Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation of LGSF-SBDP and thereafter post the same in at least three conspicuous places within the locality; and

b. Facilitate the creation of LGU’s official website to bolster transparency and accountability to the Municipality’s constituents and stakeholders.

Likewise, post the aforementioned quarterly reports on the LGU’s website and notify in writing the NTF-ELCAC Vice Chairperson and RTF-ELCAC concerned, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance when the reports are already posted thereon.

We recommended the Management to:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile all outstanding contributions and remit the full amount owed to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period. To ensure accurate financial processing of transaction, the Municipal Accountant and HRMO must reconcile their records and submit all necessary information and supporting documentation;

b. Direct the HRMO to implement an adequate internal control system. This system must verify that all employees meet GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG documentary requirements, including updated identification numbers, before their first payroll. This will prevent non-remittance of premium contributions and must include regular verification and updates; and

c. Maintain open communication with GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG to address any issues promptly and ensure accurate billing and remittance.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the GAD Focal Person and GAD key personnel to undergo capacity building programs and training on GAD to enhance their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools like HGDG on the attributed budget to evaluate gender responsiveness of PPAs in the GPB;

b. Ensure that the PPAs charged to GAD fund are only those that would address the identified gender issues in the locality. The gender issues must be specific and supported by evidence such as sex-disaggregated data and gender analysis results; and

c. Ensure that all GAD PPAs are properly implemented and that no expenses must be charged to GAD funds unless directly related to the planned PPAs per GPB. Likewise, ensure that the Municipality is compliant with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 relative to the utilization of GAD funds. Moreover, ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation. Further, submit justification/explanation on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
clearly identify the reconciling items in the BRS for the 13 bank accounts and draw the necessary JEV to recognize all reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the books of accounts;
regularly submit the mandatory financial reports and the supporting documents within the reglementary period; and
The Municipal Treasurer to post the balances of the Cash in Bank- LCCA in the cashbook, with proper segregation of the sources of funds; and coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt delivery of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to facilitate the timely reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
revisit the Annual Audit Report for CY 2023 and immediately comply the necessary adjusting entries;
initiate the transfer of completed public infrastructure assets in the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
prepare JEV to correct misclassification of PPE to another account.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to enjoin:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the unremitted Pag-ibig contribution and remit immediately the balances to the concerned agency; and
The Human Resource Management Officer to intensify compliance to the requirement of MID number and MDF prior to contract renewals or hiring of new employees.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to conduct necessary adjusting entries to correct the Trust Liability- DRRM account balance; and
The Municipal Treasurer to transfer a total of ₱298,810.00 from DRRM Trust Fund account to General Fund as payment for the additional purchase of prepositioned goods that was erroneously charged to General Fund account.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The department heads concerned to fast track the completion/acquisition of the development projects, and moving forward, to ensure that all development projects are completed as of yearend;
The Municipal Engineer to conduct thorough ocular inspection, issue certificate of completion on the actual date of project completion, and ensure that the projects implemented by phase are usable upon the completion of each phase; and
The Local Budget Officer to post the accurate utilization reports that are consistent with the reports submitted by the Engineering Department.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The Municipal Engineer to implement strict monitoring mechanisms to ensure the completion of projects within target dates, and reports on the status of project/program implementation are regularly prepared; and
The Municipal Accountant to accurately prepare the quarterly fund utilization report duly validated by both the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Treasurer, and ensure its subsequent posting in at least 3 conspicuous public places.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct the:
Local School Board to:
Furnish copy of the approved SEF Budget within 7 days from the date of its approval to the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Budget Officer;
Submit copy of the Annual and Quarterly SEF utilization report to the DepEd CO, through DepEd SDS and furnish copy of the reports to the Sangguniang Bayan and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the utilization reports to be uploaded in the DILG FDP Portal, duly validated and reconciled with the records of the Municipal Budget Office, and copy furnished the LSB Secretary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents to the audit team for further auditorial review.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted.
We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the agency, agreed to spearhead the preparation and the submission of the AAPSI CY 2022 and prior years with much urgency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:

the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables; and

the Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) the correct basis is used to book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, (ii) properly record the transactions pertaining to the collections of property taxes, and (iii) analyze the entries provided in order to rectify the noted variances of the affected accounts. 

Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant should always follow the correct accounting entries in order to reflect the correct amounts of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balance of the Trust Liabilities and provide sufficient and necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account, complete with the special account subsidiary ledger, trial balance and financial statements for the operation of municipality’s local economic enterprise.

Direct the Inventory Committee to facilitate conduct of complete physical count of the Agency’s assets pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. The RPCPPE must be submitted not later than January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor, and the same must be reconciled with the records of the Accountant;
observations.managementAction: Management to hasten the conduct of physical count and RPCPPE will be completely prepared and submitted thereafter.
observations.reason: Incomplete conduct of physical count of PPE and RPCPPE were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to properly maintain Stock Cards, Property Cards, SLC and PPELC and conduct periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records to come up with accurate reports;
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel will be requested to be assigned in the Treasury and Accounting Offices.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to prepare and maintain the required records.

observations.recommendation: Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, strictly in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with a reliable PPE balance that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management to consider the creation of a   separate Inventory Committee for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.reason: Physical count of all the LGU’s properties was not yet completed as of year-end

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory by adopting the Perpetual Inventory System and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and expense accounts;
observations.managementAction: Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to implement Perpetual Inventory System.

observations.recommendation: Require all the Inventory Custodians to ensure that all issuances of supplies and materials are covered by RIS and the Municipal Treasurer to use the RIS as basis for the preparation of the SSMI. observations.managementAction: Additional personnel will be requested to be assigned in the Treasury and Accounting Offices.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to: i) deposit immediately all collections on hand and ensure that all subsequent collections are deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day to the AGDB pursuant to the pertinent regulations on the receipts and deposit of government monies; and ii) update her cashbook regularly to come up with the correct Cash – Local Treasury account balance; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will see to it that deposits of collections will be made immediately on subsequent transactions and that entries in the cashbooks are checked.
observations.reason: Distance of the Municipality to the nearest AGDB was the primary reason for delayed deposits.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Subsidiary Ledger on the Cash–Local Treasury account, determine the proper AOs with undeposited collections, conduct immediate reconciliation of records together with the Municipal Treasurer and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: SL was already prepared but the balances pertaining to  AOs and/or collecting clerks were still not completely identified.
observations.reason: SL was already prepared but the balances pertaining to  AOs and/or collecting clerks were still not completely identified.

observations.recommendation: Management require the AOs in the MTO to strictly comply with the timely remittance/deposit of their collections as provided in Section 29 Volume I of the Manual of the NGAS for LGUs. New stub of accountable forms must be issued to Collecting Clerks only when collections from prior stubs issued to them were already remitted in full;
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel will be assigned to assist in the preparation of RCDs.
observations.reason: The late remittance of collections was caused by the delayed preparation of RCDs.

observations.recommendation: The AOs exercise diligence in the preparation of RCD and recording of transactions in the cashbook to avoid complexity and discrepancies with other records;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will see to it that entries in the cashbooks are checked.
observations.reason: Inadvertence on the part of the collecting clerks.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant prepare Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) per AO to show the details of collections and remittances as support for the Cash-Local Treasury account; and
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Increase the Fidelity Bond of the AOs in accordance with their respective total accountabilities and in compliance with Item 5.1 of Treasury Circular No. 02-009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management will determine how the cost of increased bond coverage will be allocated.
observations.reason: Fidelity Bonds of AOs were not yet increased.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers or refund the amount advanced, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who were unable to comply with the requirement;
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit. Please see Part II of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to continue to monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances and regularly send written demand on the submission of liquidation report or refund thereof.

observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit. Please see Part II of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances unless the previous ones are fully settled; and
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2023 Audit. Please see Part II of this Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: File a request for authority to write-off dormant or long outstanding unliquidated cash advances to the Office of the Auditor following the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Accounting office to gather pertinent documents/information to support their request to write-off dormant or long outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to exert more effort in preparing and submitting the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We request that such activities be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting;
observations.managementAction: The LCE required the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted DVs with complete supporting documents the soonest possible time; and
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Treasurer promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Submission of DVs was not given priority due to workloads of the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the 45 cancelled checks for validation and henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein. Likewise, prudence must be exercised in encoding the details of the checks in order to avoid wastage of checks/resources.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Treasurer promised to comply with the recommendation.

observations.reason: Delays were still incurred in the implementation of some of the PPAs.

Also, the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the budget based on the duly approved AIP indicating each PPA specifically and identify the expected outputs for each PPAs with their corresponding appropriation. For those appropriations already presented in lump sum that are not yet implemented, a separate SB appropriation ordinance be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government;

observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Submit the warranty security as support to the DVs on final payments, if available, otherwise, require the contractors to post warranty security for all completed and accepted projects.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Some of the final payments’ warranty security were not attached to its DV.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that planned projects/programs/activities for SEF are optimally implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents in accordance with the proper utilization of the fund;
observations.managementAction: Management to direct the LSB to exert more effort in ensuring that PPAs for SEF are carefully planned and implemented on time for the benefits of the constituents, most especially the students.
observations.reason: SEF budget for CY 2023 was not planned according to the prescribed timelines and not fully utilized as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: Require the liquidation of financial assistance granted to DepEd personnel and refrain from granting the same. Instead, the Municipality can provide the supplies and materials needs of the agency/different schools in accordance with the guidelines, procurement law and accounting procedures to effectively monitor and determine whether or not the funds were utilized for its intended purpose; and
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still granted to some DepEd personnel.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements are recorded and proper accounts are used in the recording of the financial transactions and supporting documents thereof are complete.

observations.recommendation: Render an explanation or justification on the resort to alternative method of procurement; and

observations.recommendation: Submit the related requisition/issue slips and duly accomplished supporting documents to the GSO or Municipal Treasurer, as the case may be, and Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate evaluation, recording and posting of receipts and issuances/utilization of COVID-19 vaccines in the books and property records of the Municipality; and

observations.reason: Laxity in complying with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging prior years’ expenses against current appropriation. Appropriation ordinance of the current year must only be disbursed for expenditures incurred on that year.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Budget Officer promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Laxity in complying with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of granting cash assistance to clients or person in the absence of complete documentary requirements. The submission of complete documentary requirements for each type of assistance are condition precedent to the payment; and
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Laxity in complying with the audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: The concerned official will seek assistance to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that catering services, if duly funded as part of PPAs of the LGU, must be made in modest amount and not unnecessary and excessive; and
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The said provision was still not observed in CY 2023 for catering services payments.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of granting snacks and meals for regular meeting of departments, councils and committees, otherwise, charge the catering expenses to the representation allowances of LGU officials hosting the said activities.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Non-compliance of the concerned officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to submit the necessary documentary requirements to support the payments for honoraria. observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to facilitate the conduct of complete physical count of all the Municipality’s properties annually and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor;
Facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain property records for all PPE and undertake the required reconciliation of the Property and Accounting records thereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention in the preparation and submission of the required BRS, maintain SL per bank account, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the FS and other documents; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Source Agencies on the status of fund transfers to the Municipality and close the accounts that have become dormant and revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case maybe, if the said accounts represent excess of the expenses for the projects funded by NGAs and/or other Agencies.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in retrieving the previous years’ records to verify the balances of the Due to NGAs account and continue the preparation and maintenance of SLs and records of the Agency accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate the balances in the FS.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and demand from the Former Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all their undeposited collections and strictly adhere with the guidelines relative to collections and deposits;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLs per collecting officer and ensure regular reconciliation of the same with the records of the Municipal Treasurer; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, the Liquidating Officers and the collectors to deposit/turnover all subsequent collections intact and not to retain portion of them pursuant to the aforecited provisions.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each account in their respective fund classification;
Prepare and maintain SLs for the aforementioned reciprocal accounts; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entry adjustments to correct the erroneous entry made for cash advance for CF;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the complete required supporting documents on the grant of cash advance for CF amounting to ₱200,000.00 to COA ATL. Likewise, the SDO must prepare and submit to ICFAU the Liquidation Report supported with complete documentary requirements in accordance with the guidelines provided in the aforementioned JC; and
Strengthen the control over the subsequent cash advance of the agency, relating to Confidential and Intelligence Fund, by strictly conforming to the provisions of the aforementioned JC before disbursing funds.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Accountant to submit the complete required documents to support the payments for consultancy services pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and henceforth, see to it that payments or disbursements are supported with adequate documentation. Further, the Municipal Accountant ensure the proper withholding of taxes from payment of consultancy services.

We recommended that Management:
Stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 in all procurements;
Render an explanation or justification on the resort to alternative methods of procurement instead of competitive bidding in the purchase of various goods and services; and
Direct the Municipal Official/Employees concerned to explain the other deficiencies noted on the particular disbursements and submit the lacking supporting documents for each transaction.

We recommended that Management observe the stipulations stated in the MOA to ensure that the projects are implemented and completed as programmed. Further, the BAC require the supplier to deliver the goods as soon as possible or terminate the contract, if warranted. Moreover, Management strengthen its fiscal management and properly implement and utilize funds granted to the Municipality in order for stakeholders to enjoy the optimum benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to cover all the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in order to enhance the preparedness of the Municipality against disasters and avoid, lessen or limit their adverse impacts to the community. Further, ensure that the prepared reports for LDRRMF are consistent, complete and compliant to the provisions stated in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management be more prudent in the utilization of its LDRRMF, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.
Further, we recommended that the LCE:
Direct the MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP and utilize the LDRRMF for tangible projects and activities that will help build disaster resilient communities and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality. Moreover, ensure that the prepared reports for LDRRMF are complete and compliant to the provisions stated in COA Circular 2012-002;
Require the MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the administrative expenses of MDRRMO Office chargeable against MOOE in the GF; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents for each transaction and ensure that documentary requirements for subsequent transactions are complete prior to any disbursement of government funds.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC Chairperson to immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the required supporting documents to avoid audit suspension of payments for the infrastructure projects implemented by the Municipality. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the DVs before payments for infrastructure projects are made.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the BAC to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review.
Further, direct the personnel responsible for accepting deliveries to notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries, furnish copies of AIRs within 24 hours upon acceptance and submit report on deliveries received, issued to end-users and remaining balance as of December 31, year-end.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs, ORs including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and
Direct the concerned officials that processing of vouchers be only undertaken to those which were properly approved/signed and with complete supporting documents.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
record accurately the cash advances and the corresponding liquidations;
determine the specific transaction/s comprising the ₱59,412.20 unliquidated cash advance of the former Municipal Treasurer and demand the immediate liquidation thereof; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer to refrain from transferring the custody of cash to the MPDC or anybody else, and ensure full control and custody of the cash advance.
The Municipal Accountant accepted the deficiencies noted and promised to comply with the provided audit recommendations.  Correspondingly, the Acting Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the observation and agreed in refraining the transfer of cash custody to other municipal personnel.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to take the initial step in confirming the status of the cash advances and to direct the Municipal Accountant to forward the result of the confirmation to the audit team and consequently record the liquidation in books of accounts, if warranted.
The Municipal Accountant promised to confirm with ICFAU the status of the cash advances for confidential funds.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to enjoin the full compliance of the department heads concerned with the requirements of the Circular.
The Municipal Engineer, the General Services Officer-Designate, and the Municipal Accountant will act on the audit recommendation.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
the LDRRM Officer to include the available fund totaling to ₱1,618,229.73 in the LDRRMFIP by incorporating responsive and beneficial local disaster risk reduction and management related PPAs and the budget for each PPA should be specified as to the year of unutilized balance it will be charged and not in lumpsum; and
the Municipal Accountant to prepare the correct and complete LDRRMF Utilization Report duly validated and reconciled with the records of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer and be submitted to the audit team within the reglementary period and the same be posted in the DILG’s Full Disclosure Policy Portal;
During the Exit Conference, local financial managers informed the Audit Team that the unexpended balance of the 5% LDRRMF was duly supported with actual cash back up, hence, the LDRRM Officer can incorporate PPAs related to disaster management.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant provided the audit justification on the difference of ₱1,617,863.58 in the charges made to the special trust fund, however, the Audit Team reminded the Municipal Accountant that sufficient disclosures in the Notes to FS should be made for transparency of government transactions.
In the same manner, the management agreed to collaborate in order to come up with accurate and consistent reports to be submitted to oversight agencies.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed  to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to (a) require all collectors to prepare and submit the RAAF incorporating therein the received, issued and cancelled accountable forms with and without face value; (b) submit all obsolete bank checks accompanied by inventory reports; (c) ensure that all collections made are duly acknowledged by issuing the appropriate accountable forms; and (d) impose internal control measures for the immediate remittance of collections.
The concerned personnel promised the Audit Team that they will exert much effort in order to provide full compliance on the audit recommendations.  Further, the Audit Team will strictly monitor the compliance of the management as the same audit observation was noted referencing to the Annual Audit Report CY 2021-6.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The BAC Secretariat to submit within the reglementary period all perfected contracts for the timely auditorial review for the detection and correction of errors;
The BAC Chairperson to demand from the contractor concerned to submit the identified lacking supporting documents, and moving forward, to ensure complete of documents prior to the perfection of contract agreement; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the contractor the immediate cancellation of issued official receipts and demand from the former the reissuance of new official receipt indicating therein the amount at gross income.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted.
The BAC Chairperson was amenable on the audit observation and agreed to comply with the recommendations provided.
Also, the Acting Municipal Treasurer promised to act accordingly relating to the proper issuance of official receipts by the contractor concerned.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents to the audit team for further auditorial review.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted.
On April 22, 2024, the Municipal Engineer was able to submit the documents subject of the audit observation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure that all tax remittances to BIR are duly supported with the verified Monthly Alphalist of Payees (MAP);
Remit all taxes withheld within the reglementary period to avoid possible imposition of penalty/surcharge; and
Submit lacking paid DVs together with the complete supporting documents.
The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer admitted their deficiencies and promised the Audit Team to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the agency, agreed to spearhead the preparation and the submission of the AAPSI CY 2022 and prior years with much urgency.
As of the date of writing, Management still failed to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI. The Audit Team will closely monitor the agency’s compliance on the audit recommendations to avoid recurrence of the same observation.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to record the unrecorded disbursements in SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for each
inventory item, conduct periodic physical count of inventories, and submit the
corresponding RPCI to the Audit Team complete with necessary information and
correct classification of inventory accounts; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to maintain SLCs for the inventories, record the
purchases of supplies/inventories initially under the appropriate asset account, and
recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed in
accordance with IPSAS 12.

Management’s Comments:
The Management informed the Audit Team that purchase of various supplies and materials
were not recorded in the stock cards and recorded as outright expense because the items
delivered to them were directly distributed to end users through Requisition and Issue Slips
(RIS) attached to the DVs. Such were done because the warehouse where those inventories
items are supposed to be stored is not yet constructed. Also, the Management lacks personnel
for the maintenance of SLCs.
Auditor's Rejoinder:
Management’s comments were duly noted. However, we emphasized that the non-recording
of the purchases of supplies and materials as inventory and the recording of the same as
outright expense upon receipt/delivery are contrary to the provisions of Sections 114 and 124
of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Paragraphs 9, 11 and 44 of IPSAS 12, thereby
affecting the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements which is not in
consonance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. In view thereof, we maintain the audit
recommendations and we shall keep this under review to ensure the full implementation of
the recommendations.

deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal in
accordance with existing rules and regulations.

Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts,
investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the
correct balances for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial
statements.

Management’s Comments:

of the matter. However, a creation of a new Committee is deemed necessary due to the
retirement of its Chairperson, Engr. Alaurin.

and Disposal Committee be created. As regards the other recommendations, compliance
therewith shall be monitored by the Audit Team to ensure the full implementation of the
recommendations.

     further deterioration and to  generate  additional  income  from  their
     disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:


          ➢ Municipal  Treasurer  to  intensify  collections  of  the  Loans
            Receivables, designate a key  personnel  who  will  monitor  the
            loans granted and  enforce  payment  thereof  and  to  institute
            legal actions against the debtors.

            beneficiaries to help enforce collection.

      filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance  with  COA  Circular

      pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 9, 2016.

      Accountant to submit to the Auditor  the  Travel  Authority  from  the
      Undersecretary  for  Local  Government,  Director,  Bureau  of   Local
      Government Supervision and/or DILG Regional Director, if there is any,

      Travel Authorization from the  Undersecretary  for  Local  Government,
      Director, Bureau of Local Government Supervision and/or DILG  Regional
      Director  prior  to  departure  in  compliance  with  DILG  Memorandum
      Circular No. 2006-22.

      require the Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  complete  sets  of

      Accountant to submit to the Audit Team  all  the  required  supporting

   approval thereof for timely verification and review of the same  pursuant

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

            • require the Municipal Planning  and  Development  Coordinator
              (MPDC)  to  ensure  that  the  mandatory   Development   Fund
              appropriated out of the Internal Revenue Allotment  be  fully
              utilized  to  implement  prioritized   identified   programs,

              1.


            • install regular monitoring mechanism to assess  the  progress

              development  projects could  be  implemented  immediately  to
              ensure that  intended  beneficiaries  avail  of  the  maximum
              benefits that will be derived therefrom.


            •  ensure  that  a  closer  coordination  among  the  officials
              involved in planning, execution and monitoring is enhanced to
              achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  the  members  of
      the Local School Board to:

     a. prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted  efforts  of
        observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements  on
        SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization;


     b. refrain from accommodating expenditures for  activities  for which
        the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;


     c. request for input from the  DepEd  officials  or  Schools  Division
        Superintendent  in  the  prioritization   of   SEF   projects   and
        expenditures in accordance with the DepEd  criteria  and  with  due
        regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing
        the limitations on the use of the fund;


     d. maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its  objectives,  monitor
        the implementation of the projects/programs and activities  (PPAs),
        and take appropriate actions such as realignment or  reprogramming,
        if necessary.

observations.reason: Lack of GSO Personnel. Steps in disposal of property to be undertaken. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Notes to Financial Statements include disclosures showing the: Changes in budgets; Material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; Reconciliation between the SCBAA and SAAOB balances and; Reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements which will guide the users/readers in making sound financial decision.
observations.managementAction: Disclosures regarding the reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, reconciliation between the SCBAA and SAAOB balances, changes in budgets and material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts were provided.
